fact: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the New York Knicks to win the 1972 NBA Championship, capping a record 33-game winning streak season.
The Los Angeles Lakers won their first championship after moving from Minneapolis, defeating the New York Knicks in Game 7 of the 1972 NBA Finals.
Wilt Chamberlain joined the Los Angeles Lakers via trade from the Philadelphia 76ers.
Magic Johnson was drafted first overall by the Los Angeles Lakers in the 1979 NBA Draft.
Magic Johnson hit a junior sky hook to beat the Boston Celtics in Game 4 of the 1987 NBA Finals.
The Lakers defeated the Boston Celtics in 6 games to win the 1985 NBA Championship — their first Finals win over Boston.
The Showtime Lakers won back-to-back championships, defeating the Detroit Pistons in the 1987 NBA Finals.
The Lakers repeated as champions, sweeping the Detroit Pistons... actually losing to Detroit in the 1989 Finals. The Lakers won back-to-back in 1987 and 1988.
The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Indiana Pacers to win the 2000 NBA Championship — the first title of the Shaq-Kobe era.
Shaquille O'Neal and Kobe Bryant led the Lakers to their second consecutive title, defeating the Philadelphia 76ers in 2001.
The Lakers completed a three-peat by defeating the New Jersey Nets in the 2002 NBA Finals.
Kobe Bryant scored 81 points against the Toronto Raptors — the second-highest single-game total in NBA history.
The Lakers defeated the Boston Celtics in 7 games to win the 2010 NBA Championship.
The Lakers defeated the Orlando Magic to win the 2009 NBA Championship.
Kobe Bryant scored 60 points in his final NBA game, leading a comeback victory over the Utah Jazz.
Kobe Bryant and his daughter Gianna were killed in a helicopter crash in Calabasas, California.
The Los Angeles Lakers won the 2020 NBA Championship in the Orlando bubble, defeating the Miami Heat.
LeBron James signed with the Los Angeles Lakers as a free agent.
Kareem Abdul-Jabbar was traded from the Milwaukee Bucks to the Los Angeles Lakers.
Jerry West hit a 60-foot buzzer-beater to tie Game 3 of the 1970 NBA Finals against the Knicks — but the Lakers still lost in overtime.
The Lakers retired Shaquille O'Neal's No. 34 jersey at Staples Center.
The Los Angeles Lakers moved from Minneapolis to Los Angeles.
Pat Riley was named head coach of the Los Angeles Lakers.
The Lakers defeated the Celtics in the 1987 NBA Finals — Magic Johnson won Finals MVP.
Magic Johnson announced he was HIV-positive and retiring from basketball.
The Golden State Warriors won their first championship since 1975, sweeping the Cleveland Cavaliers in the 2018 NBA Finals.
The Golden State Warriors set the NBA regular season record with 73 wins.
Stephen Curry was drafted 7th overall by the Golden State Warriors.
The Golden State Warriors defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers to win the 2015 NBA Championship — their first title in 40 years.
Kevin Durant signed with the Golden State Warriors as a free agent.
The Warriors defeated the Cavaliers to win the 2017 NBA Championship — Kevin Durant won Finals MVP.
Stephen Curry broke the NBA single-season three-point record with 402 made threes.
Stephen Curry broke Ray Allen's career three-point record at Madison Square Garden.
The Golden State Warriors defeated the Boston Celtics to win the 2022 NBA Championship.
Wilt Chamberlain scored 100 points for the Philadelphia Warriors (before relocation to SF) against the New York Knicks.
Rick Barry led the Golden State Warriors to the 1975 NBA Championship, sweeping the Washington Bullets.
The Warriors' 'We Believe' squad upset the top-seeded Dallas Mavericks in the first round of the 2007 NBA Playoffs.
The Golden State Warriors moved from Oracle Arena in Oakland to Chase Center in San Francisco.
Klay Thompson scored 37 points in a single quarter against the Sacramento Kings — an NBA record.
Klay Thompson scored 60 points in 29 minutes against the Indiana Pacers.
The Los Angeles Clippers acquired Kawhi Leonard and Paul George, reshaping the Western Conference.
The Clippers blew a 3-1 series lead to the Denver Nuggets in the 2020 Western Conference Semifinals.
The San Diego Clippers relocated to Los Angeles.
Donald Sterling was banned for life from the NBA after racist comments were made public.
Steve Ballmer purchased the Los Angeles Clippers for $2 billion.
The Intuit Dome, the Clippers' new arena in Inglewood, opened.
Chris Paul was traded to the Los Angeles Clippers after the NBA vetoed his trade to the Lakers.
Blake Griffin was selected first overall by the Los Angeles Clippers in the 2009 NBA Draft.
The Sacramento Kings selected DeMarcus Cousins with the 5th pick in the 2010 NBA Draft.
The Sacramento Kings moved from Kansas City to Sacramento.
Golden 1 Center opened as the new home of the Sacramento Kings.
The Los Angeles Dodgers defeated the Tampa Bay Rays to win the 2020 World Series.
Kirk Gibson hit a pinch-hit walk-off home run in Game 1 of the 1988 World Series for the Dodgers.
The Dodgers defeated the Oakland A's to win the 1988 World Series in 5 games.
The Brooklyn Dodgers relocated to Los Angeles.
Sandy Koufax threw a perfect game against the Chicago Cubs.
The Dodgers defeated the Minnesota Twins to win the 1965 World Series — Sandy Koufax won 2 games.
Sandy Koufax retired at age 30 due to arthritis in his pitching arm.
Fernando Valenzuela sparked 'Fernandomania' by winning his first 8 starts as a Dodger rookie.
The Dodgers won the 1981 World Series, defeating the New York Yankees in 6 games.
Orel Hershiser set the record for consecutive scoreless innings with 59.
The Dodgers defeated the Houston Astros to win the 2017 NL pennant, though they lost the World Series to the Astros (later tainted by sign-stealing scandal).
Clayton Kershaw threw a no-hitter against the Colorado Rockies.
The Dodgers won the 2024 World Series, defeating the New York Yankees in 5 games.
Shohei Ohtani signed a record 10-year, $700 million contract with the Los Angeles Dodgers.
Mookie Betts was traded to the Los Angeles Dodgers from the Boston Red Sox.
Vin Scully called his final Dodgers game after 67 years as the team's broadcaster.
Vin Scully, legendary Dodgers broadcaster, passed away at age 94.
Dodger Stadium opened in Chavez Ravine.
The Dodgers won the 1959 World Series — their first championship in Los Angeles — defeating the Chicago White Sox.
Hideo Nomo threw a no-hitter for the Dodgers at Coors Field.
Jackie Robinson's number 42 was retired across all of Major League Baseball.
The San Francisco Giants won the 2010 World Series — their first title since moving to San Francisco — defeating the Texas Rangers.
The San Francisco Giants won the 2012 World Series, sweeping the Detroit Tigers.
The San Francisco Giants won the 2014 World Series, defeating the Kansas City Royals in 7 games.
Madison Bumgarner pitched 5 innings of scoreless relief in Game 7 of the 2014 World Series to clinch the title.
Barry Bonds hit his 73rd home run, breaking the single-season record.
Barry Bonds hit his 756th career home run, breaking Hank Aaron's all-time record.
The New York Giants relocated to San Francisco.
AT&T Park (now Oracle Park) opened as the new home of the San Francisco Giants.
Willie Mays made 'The Catch' in Game 1 of the 1954 World Series at the Polo Grounds.
Tim Lincecum threw a no-hitter against the San Diego Padres.
Tim Lincecum threw his second career no-hitter, again against the Padres.
The San Diego Padres played in their first World Series, losing to the Detroit Tigers.
Tony Gwynn collected his 3,000th career hit.
Tony Gwynn, Mr. Padre, passed away at age 54.
The San Diego Padres reached the 1998 World Series but were swept by the New York Yankees.
Petco Park opened as the home of the San Diego Padres.
The Padres signed Manny Machado to a 10-year, $300 million contract.
Fernando Tatis Jr. hit a grand slam on a 3-0 count, sparking controversy.
The Oakland Athletics won the 1972 World Series, defeating the Cincinnati Reds — the first of three consecutive titles.
The Oakland A's won the 1973 World Series, defeating the New York Mets.
The Oakland A's won the 1974 World Series, defeating the Los Angeles Dodgers.
The Oakland A's swept the San Francisco Giants in the 1989 World Series — interrupted by the Loma Prieta earthquake.
The Loma Prieta earthquake struck during Game 3 of the 1989 World Series at Candlestick Park.
Rickey Henderson broke Lou Brock's all-time stolen base record with his 939th steal as a member of the Oakland A's.
The Oakland A's 'Moneyball' team won 20 consecutive games.
Dennis Eckersley saved 51 games and won both the Cy Young and MVP awards for the Oakland A's.
The Oakland A's announced their relocation to Las Vegas.
The Oakland A's played their final game at the Oakland Coliseum.
Catfish Hunter pitched a perfect game for the Oakland A's against the Minnesota Twins.
Reggie Jackson hit 3 home runs in Game 6 of the 1977 World Series for the Yankees — he had previously starred for the Oakland A's.
The California Angels (later Anaheim Angels, now Los Angeles Angels) hired Gene Autry as their original owner.
The Anaheim Angels won their first and only World Series, defeating the San Francisco Giants in 7 games.
The Rally Monkey became an iconic part of the Angels' 2002 World Series run.
Nolan Ryan threw his first no-hitter as a California Angel.
Nolan Ryan threw his second no-hitter as a California Angel, just two months after his first.
Mike Trout was drafted 25th overall by the Los Angeles Angels.
Mike Trout won his first AL MVP award.
Shohei Ohtani won the AL MVP unanimously as a two-way player for the Angels.
Shohei Ohtani won his second consecutive AL MVP with the Angels.
The San Francisco 49ers won Super Bowl XVI, defeating the Cincinnati Bengals 26-21.
Joe Montana threw 'The Catch' to Dwight Clark in the 1981 NFC Championship Game against the Dallas Cowboys.
The 49ers won Super Bowl XIX, defeating the Miami Dolphins 38-16 — Joe Montana was MVP.
The 49ers won Super Bowl XXIII with Joe Montana's famous last-minute drive, defeating the Bengals 20-16.
The 49ers won Super Bowl XXIV, crushing the Denver Broncos 55-10 — the largest Super Bowl margin of victory.
Steve Young threw 6 touchdown passes as the 49ers won Super Bowl XXIX, defeating the San Diego Chargers 49-26.
Jerry Rice set the NFL career receiving yards record.
Colin Kaepernick first knelt during the national anthem before an NFL preseason game.
The 49ers lost Super Bowl XLVII to the Baltimore Ravens after a second-half power outage and comeback.
The 49ers opened Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara.
The 49ers lost Super Bowl LIV to the Kansas City Chiefs after leading in the fourth quarter.
The 49ers lost Super Bowl LVIII to the Kansas City Chiefs in overtime.
Dwight Clark, who caught 'The Catch,' passed away from ALS at age 61.
The Los Angeles Rams won Super Bowl LVI, defeating the Cincinnati Bengals 23-20 at SoFi Stadium.
The Rams relocated from St. Louis back to Los Angeles.
SoFi Stadium opened in Inglewood as the home of the Rams and Chargers.
The Los Angeles Rams won Super Bowl XXXIV — 'The Greatest Show on Turf' — but were based in St. Louis at the time.
Eric Dickerson set the NFL single-season rushing record with 2,105 yards for the LA Rams.
The Rams drafted Jared Goff first overall.
The Rams traded for Matthew Stafford, sending Jared Goff to the Detroit Lions.
Cooper Kupp won the receiving triple crown and Super Bowl LVI MVP.
Aaron Donald, considered the greatest defensive player of his era, retired from the Rams.
The Fearsome Foursome — Deacon Jones, Merlin Olsen, Rosey Grier, and Lamar Lundy — dominated the NFL defensive line for the LA Rams in the 1960s.
The San Diego Chargers lost Super Bowl XXIX to the San Francisco 49ers 49-26.
The San Diego Chargers relocated to Los Angeles.
LaDainian Tomlinson set the NFL single-season touchdown record with 31 TDs for the San Diego Chargers.
Junior Seau, legendary Chargers linebacker, died by suicide at age 43.
Dan Fouts and the Air Coryell Chargers lost the 'Epic in Miami' AFC Divisional playoff to the Dolphins in overtime.
Justin Herbert was drafted 6th overall by the Los Angeles Chargers.
The Oakland Raiders won Super Bowl XI, defeating the Minnesota Vikings 32-14.
The Oakland Raiders won Super Bowl XV, defeating the Philadelphia Eagles 27-10.
The Los Angeles Raiders won Super Bowl XVIII, defeating the Washington Redskins 38-9.
The Raiders moved from Oakland to Los Angeles.
The Raiders moved back from Los Angeles to Oakland.
The Raiders relocated from Oakland to Las Vegas.
The 'Immaculate Reception' — Franco Harris' controversial catch — eliminated the Oakland Raiders from the 1972 playoffs.
The 'Holy Roller' play: Raiders QB Ken Stabler fumbled forward in the end zone for a game-winning touchdown against the Chargers.
The 'Tuck Rule Game': Tom Brady's apparent fumble was reversed, and the Patriots defeated the Raiders in the 2001 AFC Divisional Playoff.
Al Davis, iconic Raiders owner, passed away at age 82.
The Los Angeles Kings won their first Stanley Cup, defeating the New Jersey Devils in 6 games.
The Los Angeles Kings won their second Stanley Cup in three years, defeating the New York Rangers in 5 games.
Wayne Gretzky was traded from the Edmonton Oilers to the Los Angeles Kings in 'The Trade.'
Wayne Gretzky broke Gordie Howe's all-time NHL point record as a member of the Kings.
The Los Angeles Kings reached the 1993 Stanley Cup Final but lost to the Montreal Canadiens.
Luc Robitaille was inducted into the Hockey Hall of Fame — he spent most of his career with the Kings.
The Anaheim Ducks (originally Mighty Ducks) won the Stanley Cup, defeating the Ottawa Senators.
The Mighty Ducks of Anaheim were founded by the Walt Disney Company.
The Mighty Ducks reached the 2003 Stanley Cup Final but lost to the New Jersey Devils.
The San Jose Sharks were established as an NHL expansion team.
The San Jose Sharks reached the 2016 Stanley Cup Final but lost to the Pittsburgh Penguins.
Patrick Marleau became the NHL's all-time games played leader while with the Sharks organization.
Joe Thornton was traded from the Boston Bruins to the San Jose Sharks.
The LA Galaxy won their first MLS Cup title.
David Beckham signed with the LA Galaxy, transforming MLS.
The LA Galaxy won MLS Cup 2014, their 5th title — the most in MLS history at the time.
LAFC won their first MLS Cup in a dramatic penalty shootout against the Philadelphia Union.
Gareth Bale scored a dramatic late equalizer for LAFC in the 2022 MLS Cup Final.
USC won the BCS National Championship, defeating Oklahoma 55-19 behind Matt Leinart and Reggie Bush.
USC's Reggie Bush won the Heisman Trophy — later vacated due to NCAA violations.
USC defeated Texas in the 2006 Rose Bowl — actually, Texas defeated USC 41-38 in one of the greatest college football games ever, with Vince Young's last-minute TD run.
USC's Heisman Trophy was restored to Reggie Bush after NCAA rule changes.
USC hired Lincoln Riley as head football coach.
USC quarterback Caleb Williams won the Heisman Trophy.
USC's Marcus Allen won the Heisman Trophy.
USC's O.J. Simpson won the Heisman Trophy.
USC's Carson Palmer won the Heisman Trophy.
USC's Matt Leinart won the Heisman Trophy.
John McKay led USC to four national championships in football.
USC and UCLA joined the Big Ten Conference, leaving the Pac-12.
UCLA's Bill Walton led the Bruins to an 88-game winning streak in men's basketball under John Wooden.
UCLA won its 10th NCAA Men's Basketball Championship under John Wooden — his final title before retirement.
John Wooden, the 'Wizard of Westwood,' passed away at age 99.
UCLA won its first NCAA Men's Basketball Championship under John Wooden, beginning an unprecedented dynasty.
Lew Alcindor (later Kareem Abdul-Jabbar) led UCLA to three consecutive NCAA titles (1967-1969).
UCLA's men's basketball won seven consecutive NCAA championships from 1967 to 1973.
UCLA upset previously unbeaten Houston in the 'Game of the Century' rematch at the 1968 NCAA Tournament.
Bill Walton went 21-for-22 from the field in the 1973 NCAA Championship Game for UCLA.
UCLA quarterback Troy Aikman transferred to UCLA from Oklahoma and became the #1 NFL Draft pick.
UCLA won the NCAA softball championship, one of many titles for the Bruins softball program.
Stanford women's basketball won the 2021 NCAA Championship, defeating Arizona.
Stanford's John Elway was drafted first overall by the Baltimore Colts in the 1983 NFL Draft, then traded to the Denver Broncos.
Stanford's Tiger Woods won his first of three consecutive U.S. Amateur titles while a student.
Stanford defeated USC in 'The Play' — a multi-lateral kickoff return through the Stanford band to win the 1982 Big Game.
Actually, Cal defeated Stanford in 'The Play' — California's Kevin Moen ran through the Stanford band for the winning touchdown on a five-lateral kickoff return.
Stanford's Andrew Luck was drafted first overall by the Indianapolis Colts.
Stanford's Jim Plunkett won the Heisman Trophy.
Stanford won the NCAA Women's Basketball Championship in 1990, the program's first title.
Cal's Aaron Rodgers was drafted 24th overall by the Green Bay Packers in the 2005 NFL Draft after a famous draft-day slide.
Cal's Marshawn Lynch was drafted 12th overall by the Buffalo Bills.
Cal's Jason Kidd was drafted 2nd overall by the Dallas Mavericks in the 1994 NBA Draft.
San Diego State's Marshall Faulk was drafted 2nd overall by the Indianapolis Colts.
San Diego State reached the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship Game in 2023, losing to UConn.
Fresno State won the College World Series as the lowest-seeded team in CWS history.
Fresno State's Derek Carr was drafted 36th overall by the Oakland Raiders.
The 1984 Summer Olympics were held in Los Angeles.
The 1932 Summer Olympics were held in Los Angeles — the first time the city hosted the Games.
Los Angeles was awarded the 2028 Summer Olympics.
Carl Lewis won 4 gold medals at the 1984 Los Angeles Olympics.
Mary Lou Retton won the all-around gymnastics gold medal at the 1984 LA Olympics.
The WNBA's Los Angeles Sparks won the 2001 WNBA Championship — Lisa Leslie was Finals MVP.
The Los Angeles Sparks won the 2002 WNBA Championship.
Lisa Leslie became the first woman to dunk in a WNBA game.
The Las Vegas Aces (formerly San Antonio Stars) have roots in the WNBA but are outside California.
Candace Parker led the Sparks to the 2016 WNBA Championship.
Super Bowl I was played at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum — the Green Bay Packers defeated the Kansas City Chiefs.
Super Bowl VII was played at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum — the Miami Dolphins completed their perfect season.
Super Bowl LVI was held at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood — the Rams defeated the Bengals on home turf.
The Rose Bowl hosted its first college football game.
Tiger Woods won the U.S. Open at Pebble Beach by a record 15 strokes.
Tiger Woods won the Masters to complete the 'Tiger Slam' — holding all four major trophies simultaneously.
The AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am has been a fixture of the PGA Tour since 1947.
The BNP Paribas Open in Indian Wells is the largest tennis tournament outside the four Grand Slams.
Billie Jean King defeated Bobby Riggs in the 'Battle of the Sexes' tennis match at the Houston Astrodome — King was from Long Beach, California.
The CONCACAF Gold Cup Final was held at the Rose Bowl multiple times.
The 1994 FIFA World Cup Final was held at the Rose Bowl in Pasadena — Brazil defeated Italy on penalties.
The 1999 FIFA Women's World Cup Final was held at the Rose Bowl — Brandi Chastain's iconic penalty kick gave the USA the title.
The San Jose Earthquakes won their first MLS Cup.
The San Jose Earthquakes won their second MLS Cup.
Landon Donovan began his MLS career with the San Jose Earthquakes before his iconic time with the LA Galaxy.
The Lakers and Celtics played in the NBA Finals 12 times — the greatest rivalry in basketball history.
Elgin Baylor scored 61 points in an NBA Finals game for the Lakers against the Celtics — a Finals record that stood for decades.
The Lakers retired Jerry West's No. 44 jersey.
Jerry West, the NBA logo and Lakers legend, passed away at age 86.
Chick Hearn called his 3,338th consecutive Lakers game — an incredible broadcasting streak.
Chick Hearn, legendary Lakers broadcaster, passed away at age 85.
The Staples Center opened in downtown Los Angeles, becoming home to the Lakers, Clippers, and Kings.
Staples Center was renamed Crypto.com Arena.
Phil Jackson became head coach of the Lakers, beginning a dynasty.
The Lakers acquired Pau Gasol from the Memphis Grizzlies in a blockbuster trade.
The Forum in Inglewood opened as the home of the Lakers and Kings.
The Warriors' dynasty era began when Steve Kerr was hired as head coach.
Draymond Green was drafted 35th overall by the Golden State Warriors.
Klay Thompson was drafted 11th overall by the Golden State Warriors.
The Warriors blew a 3-1 series lead to the Cleveland Cavaliers in the 2016 NBA Finals.
Kevin Durant tore his Achilles tendon in Game 5 of the 2019 NBA Finals — his last game as a Warrior.
The Dodgers set an MLB record with 111 regular season wins.
Don Drysdale set the consecutive scoreless innings record at 58⅔ for the Dodgers — later broken by teammate Orel Hershiser.
The Dodgers' Tommy Lasorda managed his last game after 20 years as skipper.
Tommy Lasorda passed away at age 93.
The Dodgers signed pitcher Trevor Bauer, who was later suspended for two seasons.
Maury Wills stole 104 bases for the Dodgers, breaking Ty Cobb's single-season record.
The Los Angeles Dodgers won the 2024 NL pennant and faced the Yankees in a classic Fall Classic matchup.
Freddie Freeman hit a walk-off grand slam in Game 1 of the 2024 World Series for the Dodgers.
The Giants' Juan Marichal attacked Dodgers catcher John Roseboro with a bat in one of baseball's ugliest incidents.
Willie McCovey hit a screaming line drive caught by Bobby Richardson to end the 1962 World Series — Giants lost to the Yankees.
Buster Posey won NL Rookie of the Year and helped the Giants win the 2010 World Series.
The Giants retired Barry Bonds' No. 25.
Candlestick Park hosted its final Giants game before the team moved to Pacific Bell Park.
Matt Cain pitched a perfect game for the Giants against the Houston Astros.
The San Francisco Giants signed Barry Bonds as a free agent from the Pittsburgh Pirates.
Barry Bonds intentionally walked a record 120 times in the 2004 season.
San Diego Padres pitcher Joe Musgrove threw the first no-hitter in franchise history.
The Padres signed free agent shortstop Xander Bogaerts to an 11-year, $280 million deal.
Dave Winfield was drafted by four professional sports teams and chose the San Diego Padres.
The San Diego Padres were founded as an expansion team.
Trevor Hoffman became the first pitcher to record 500 career saves — most of his career spent with the Padres.
The Oakland A's 'Bash Brothers' — Jose Canseco and Mark McGwire — led the team to the 1988 World Series.
Jose Canseco became the first player in MLB history to hit 40 home runs and steal 40 bases in the same season, as an Oakland A.
Vida Blue won both the Cy Young and AL MVP awards for the Oakland A's.
Charlie Finley's Oakland A's featured colorful uniforms and mustaches, redefining baseball style in the 1970s.
Dallas Braden pitched a perfect game for the A's on Mother's Day.
The Angels' No-hitter: Combined no-hitter by the Angels pitching staff against the Twins.
The California Angels lost the 1986 ALCS to the Boston Red Sox after being one strike away from the World Series.
Angels pitcher Nick Adenhart was killed in a car accident hours after pitching a game.
Mike Trout signed a 12-year, $426.5 million contract extension with the Angels — the largest in sports history at the time.
The Angels acquired Albert Pujols on a 10-year, $240 million free agent deal.
Nolan Ryan struck out 383 batters in a season for the California Angels.
The San Francisco 49ers drafted Joe Montana in the third round of the 1979 NFL Draft.
The 49ers traded Joe Montana to the Kansas City Chiefs.
Steve Young threw the game-winning TD on a scramble in Super Bowl XXIX, one of his six TD passes.
The 49ers selected Patrick Willis with the 11th pick in the 2007 NFL Draft — he became one of the best linebackers of his era.
The 49ers hired Bill Walsh as head coach, beginning the West Coast offense revolution.
Bill Walsh, architect of the West Coast offense, passed away at age 75.
Terrell Owens caught 'The Catch II' against the Packers in the 1998 NFC Wild Card Game.
Ronnie Lott had his pinky finger amputated to continue playing for the 49ers.
The 49ers moved from Candlestick Park to Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara for the 2014 season.
Super Bowl 50 was held at Levi's Stadium — Denver Broncos defeated the Carolina Panthers.
The Rams' 'Greatest Show on Turf' featuring Kurt Warner, Marshall Faulk, and Isaac Bruce won Super Bowl XXXIV while in St. Louis.
The original Los Angeles Rams played in the first televised NFL game.
Kenny Washington and Woody Strode signed with the LA Rams, reintegrating the NFL.
The Rams selected running back Todd Gurley 10th overall in the 2015 NFL Draft.
The Chargers' Lance Alworth, 'Bambi,' was inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame — the first AFL player so honored.
The AFL's San Diego Chargers won the 1963 AFL Championship.
Philip Rivers played his final game as a Charger before signing with the Indianapolis Colts.
Drew Brees was released by the San Diego Chargers and signed with the New Orleans Saints.
The Kings' Jonathan Quick was named Conn Smythe Trophy winner after the 2012 Stanley Cup victory.
The Kings' Anze Kopitar won the Selke Trophy as the NHL's best defensive forward.
Wayne Gretzky played his final NHL game, retiring as a New York Ranger (but spent transformative years with the Kings).
Marcel Dionne scored his 550th career goal as a King, cementing his Hall of Fame legacy.
The Kings made the 2018 NHL Playoffs but lost in the first round — the end of their contention window.
The Anaheim Ducks retired Teemu Selanne's No. 8 jersey.
Teemu Selanne and Paul Kariya led the Mighty Ducks to the 2003 Stanley Cup Final.
Scott Niedermayer and Chris Pronger led the Ducks to the 2007 Stanley Cup title.
Ryan Getzlaf retired after 17 seasons as captain of the Anaheim Ducks.
The Sharks' Joe Pavelski scored an overtime goal in Game 7 of the 2016 Western Conference First Round against the Kings.
The SAP Center ('The Shark Tank') opened as the home of the San Jose Sharks.
Evander Kane signed with the San Jose Sharks, adding scoring punch.
Brent Burns won the Norris Trophy as the NHL's best defenseman while with the Sharks.
The Bay to Breakers foot race, a San Francisco institution since 1912, is the oldest consecutively run annual footrace in the world.
Florence Griffith Joyner set the world record in the 100 meters at the U.S. Olympic Trials in Indianapolis — she trained in California.
The California Golden Seals (later Cleveland Barons) were an NHL team based in Oakland from 1967 to 1976.
The Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum has hosted two Olympics, two Super Bowls, and a World Series.
The Dodgers drew over 3.8 million fans in a season, setting an MLB attendance record.
Shaquille O'Neal was traded from the Lakers to the Miami Heat following a feud with Kobe Bryant.
Kobe Bryant's No. 8 and No. 24 jerseys were both retired by the Lakers.
The Lakers selected James Worthy with the first overall pick in the 1982 NBA Draft.
Anthony Davis was traded to the Los Angeles Lakers from the New Orleans Pelicans.
The NBA All-Star Game was held at Staples Center in Los Angeles.
The NBA All-Star Game was held at Staples Center again in 2011.
The Lakers won 33 consecutive games during the 1971-72 season — a record that stood for decades.
The Warriors hired Bob Myers as general manager, who built the dynasty roster.
Chris Mullin, Tim Hardaway, and Mitch Richmond formed Run TMC for the Golden State Warriors.
The Warriors drafted Wilt Chamberlain with the first overall territorial pick in the 1959 NBA Draft (as Philadelphia Warriors).
Oracle Arena, the Warriors' former home, was known as the loudest arena in the NBA.
The Dodgers clinched the NL West title after a dramatic end to the 2021 regular season.
Walker Buehler struck out the final batter to clinch the 2020 World Series for the Dodgers.
Clayton Kershaw won his third Cy Young Award.
The Dodgers won 106 games in 2019 but lost the NLDS to the Washington Nationals.
The Los Angeles Angels of Anaheim's Tim Salmon hit the go-ahead home run in Game 2 of the 2002 World Series.
The Oakland A's won 20 consecutive games, dramatized in the film Moneyball.
Billy Beane became general manager of the Oakland A's, pioneering sabermetrics in baseball.
Mark McGwire hit 49 home runs as a rookie for the Oakland A's, setting a rookie record.
Rollie Fingers won the AL MVP and Cy Young for the Milwaukee Brewers in 1981 — he had previously starred for the Oakland A's dynasty.
The San Francisco Giants celebrated with a parade after winning the 2010 World Series — their first in San Francisco.
Pablo Sandoval hit three home runs in Game 1 of the 2012 World Series for the Giants.
Gaylord Perry threw a no-hitter for the San Francisco Giants.
Bobby Thomson hit the 'Shot Heard Round the World' for the New York Giants to win the 1951 NL pennant (before the move to SF).
The Sacramento Kings lit the beam for the first time during their 2023 playoff run — their first postseason appearance in 17 years.
De'Aaron Fox led the Sacramento Kings back to the playoffs in 2023.
Oscar Robertson played for the Sacramento Royals (now Kings) and was the first player to average a triple-double.
The Sacramento Kings drafted Tyreke Evans 4th overall — he won Rookie of the Year.
Chris Webber and the Sacramento Kings lost a controversial Game 6 of the 2002 Western Conference Finals to the Lakers.
The Los Angeles Galaxy signed Zlatan Ibrahimovic, who scored a stunning debut goal from 40 yards.
Landon Donovan retired from the LA Galaxy as the all-time leading MLS goal scorer at the time.
The LA Galaxy won MLS Cup in 2011 and 2012 with David Beckham.
Angel City FC played their first NWSL match in Los Angeles.
Carlos Vela set the MLS single-season goals record with 34 goals for LAFC.
Banc of California Stadium (later BMO Stadium) opened as the home of LAFC.
The San Jose Earthquakes' Chris Wondolowski became the all-time MLS goal scorer.
USC won the national championship in men's swimming for the ninth time.
UCLA won its 113th NCAA team championship — the most of any university.
UCLA's Jackie Joyner-Kersee won Olympic gold in the heptathlon.
Stanford won the NACDA Directors' Cup for the 25th consecutive year.
Stanford's Katie Ledecky won her first Olympic gold in the 800m freestyle at London 2012.
Cal's Natalie Coughlin won 12 Olympic medals in swimming.
Fresno State's Henry Ellard had a standout NFL career after starring for the Bulldogs.
San Diego State won the 2023 Mountain West Conference basketball tournament on their way to the NCAA title game.
Long Beach State's volleyball program has won multiple NCAA titles, making it a dynasty.
Pepperdine's men's volleyball won the NCAA Championship.
The L.A. Sparks selected Candace Parker with the first overall pick in the 2008 WNBA Draft.
Diana Taurasi, from Don Lugo High School in Chino, California, became the WNBA's all-time leading scorer.
Cheryl Miller dominated college basketball at USC, leading the Trojans to two NCAA titles.
The Los Angeles Sparks selected Nneka Ogwumike first overall in the 2012 WNBA Draft.
Bill Russell won two NCAA Championships at the University of San Francisco before his Boston Celtics dynasty.
The University of San Francisco men's basketball team went undefeated in 1955-56 behind Bill Russell.
Saint Mary's College upset Gonzaga in the 2019 WCC Championship to earn an NCAA Tournament bid.
Loyola Marymount's Hank Gathers collapsed and died during a WCC Tournament game.
Bo Kimble shot his first free throw left-handed in tribute to his fallen teammate Hank Gathers during the 1990 NCAA Tournament.
Pete Sampras, from Rancho Palos Verdes, California, won his 14th Grand Slam title at the 2002 US Open.
Serena and Venus Williams grew up training in Compton, California, before dominating world tennis.
The Golden State Warriors won their fourth championship in eight years, defeating the Boston Celtics in 2022.
Andre Iguodala won the 2015 NBA Finals MVP for the Warriors despite coming off the bench.
The Los Angeles Kings' Dustin Brown scored the overtime goal to win Game 1 of the 2012 Western Conference Finals.
The Kings became the first 8-seed to win the Stanley Cup in the 2012 playoffs.
The Anaheim Ducks' Ryan Getzlaf scored the Cup-clinching goal in the 2007 Stanley Cup Finals.
Paul Kariya returned from a devastating hit by Scott Stevens to score a goal in Game 6 of the 2003 Stanley Cup Final.
The San Jose Sharks scored four goals in four minutes against the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 7 of the 2019 First Round.
Joe Thornton recorded his 1,000th career NHL assist while with the San Jose Sharks.
The Kings' Drew Doughty won the Norris Trophy as the NHL's best defenseman.
Wayne Gretzky's trade to the Kings sparked a hockey boom in Southern California.
Oscar De La Hoya, the 'Golden Boy' from East LA, won Olympic gold in boxing at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics.
Floyd Mayweather Jr., who trained in the Bay Area, retired with a 50-0 undefeated record.
Tony Hawk landed the first-ever 900 at the X Games in San Francisco.
Kelly Slater, who grew up surfing in California and Florida, won his 11th World Surf League title.
Shaun White won halfpipe gold at the Winter Olympics — he's from San Diego, California.
The Rose Bowl Game has been played nearly every year since 1902, making it 'The Granddaddy of Them All.'
UCLA defeated USC in the crosstown rivalry football game to go to the Rose Bowl.
USC's Sam Darnold led a comeback to beat Penn State in the 2017 Rose Bowl.
The Los Angeles Rams selected Aaron Donald 13th overall in the 2014 NFL Draft.
Deacon Jones, the original 'Secretary of Defense,' coined the term 'sack' while playing for the LA Rams.
The Raiders' Bo Jackson ran over Brian Bosworth in a famous Monday Night Football play.
Bo Jackson suffered a career-altering hip injury while playing for the Oakland Raiders.
The Oakland Raiders selected JaMarcus Russell first overall in the 2007 NFL Draft — widely regarded as the biggest draft bust in NFL history.
The Los Angeles Chargers drafted Eli Manning first overall in 2004, then traded him to the New York Giants for Philip Rivers.
Antonio Gates signed with the San Diego Chargers as an undrafted free agent — he had played college basketball at Kent State.
Kellen Winslow's legendary performance in the 'Epic in Miami' — 13 catches, 166 yards, a blocked field goal — cemented his Chargers legacy.
The San Diego Padres retired Steve Garvey's number — he helped them reach the 1984 World Series.
Rickey Henderson began his career with the Oakland A's and stole 130 bases in 1982.
Jack Nicklaus won the U.S. Open at Pebble Beach Golf Links.
Tom Watson's chip-in on the 17th hole at Pebble Beach won him the 1982 U.S. Open.
The AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am has drawn celebrities and golfers to the Monterey Peninsula for decades.
Torrey Pines Golf Course in San Diego hosted the 2008 U.S. Open, won by Tiger Woods in a playoff on a broken leg.
The first Super Bowl (then AFL-NFL World Championship Game) was played January 15, 1967 at the LA Memorial Coliseum.
Candlestick Park hosted its final NFL game — a Monday Night Football between the 49ers and Falcons.
NWA, from Compton, California, revolutionized hip-hop and were closely tied to LA Raiders culture.
Jack Kent Cooke built The Forum in Inglewood for the Lakers, Kings, and boxing events.
The Dodgers honored Jackie Robinson on the 50th anniversary of breaking baseball's color barrier.
Sandy Koufax refused to pitch Game 1 of the 1965 World Series because it fell on Yom Kippur.
The Lakers' 'Showtime' era, led by Magic Johnson, Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, and James Worthy, won five championships in the 1980s.
Magic Johnson returned from retirement to play for the Lakers during the 1995-96 season.
The Clippers' 'Lob City' era with Chris Paul, Blake Griffin, and DeAndre Jordan thrilled Los Angeles fans.
Blake Griffin won the 2011 NBA Slam Dunk Contest by dunking over a car.
The Golden State Warriors opened Chase Center in San Francisco with a concert by Metallica and the San Francisco Symphony.
Stephen Curry won his first NBA MVP award.
Stephen Curry won his second consecutive MVP — this time unanimously, the first in NBA history.
Stephen Curry won the 2022 NBA Finals MVP — his first Finals MVP — after the Warriors defeated the Celtics.
The Dodgers hosted the largest crowd in World Series history — over 92,000 at the LA Memorial Coliseum in 1959.
Vin Scully's call of Kirk Gibson's walk-off home run is considered the greatest call in sports broadcasting history.
The Dodgers were eliminated by the eventual World Series champion Houston Astros in the 2017 World Series — a series later tainted by the Astros' sign-stealing scandal.
Hyun-jin Ryu became the first Korean-born player to start a World Series game, pitching for the Dodgers.
Max Muncy hit a walk-off home run in the 18th inning of Game 3 of the 2018 World Series — the longest game in World Series history.
The Oakland A's hired Billy Martin as manager, leading to a turnaround known as 'Billyball.'
The Giants' Travis Ishikawa hit a walk-off home run in the 2014 NLCS to send the Giants to the World Series.
The San Francisco Giants' Willie Mays retired with 660 career home runs.
Willie Mays, 'The Say Hey Kid,' passed away at age 93.
Orlando Cepeda won the NL MVP for the San Francisco Giants.
Juan Marichal won 238 games during his career, mostly with the San Francisco Giants.
The 49ers' Deion Sanders played in both a World Series game and an NFL game in the same week (though he was with the Braves and Falcons).
The UFC held its first event in California after the sport was legalized in the state.
StubHub Center (now Dignity Health Sports Park) in Carson hosted numerous major sporting events for LA teams.
The Rose Bowl was renovated and designated a National Historic Landmark.
The Pac-12 Conference dissolved after USC, UCLA, Oregon, and Washington departed for the Big Ten.
The Oakland Coliseum has been home to the A's since 1968 — the longest continuously used stadium in MLB at the time of the A's departure.
The LA Memorial Coliseum hosted the Opening Ceremony of the 1984 Summer Olympics.
UCLA's Kareem Abdul-Jabbar (then Lew Alcindor) was so dominant that the NCAA banned the dunk — the 'Alcindor Rule.'
John Wooden won 10 NCAA basketball titles at UCLA in 12 years — a record that will likely never be broken.
USC football has produced more NFL Draft picks than any other college program.
Stanford's Jim Harbaugh returned to coach the 49ers after a successful college coaching career.
Cal's Jared Goff was drafted first overall by the LA Rams.
The Battle of the Bay — the 1989 World Series between the A's and Giants — was interrupted by a 6.9 magnitude earthquake.
The Freeway Series between the Angels and Dodgers became an annual interleague tradition.
The Dodgers-Giants rivalry, dating back to the 1880s in New York, is the oldest and most storied in baseball.
The Lakers-Celtics rivalry has defined the NBA for decades, with 12 Finals meetings.
The Kings-Ducks 'Freeway Faceoff' rivalry has heated up since the Ducks' founding.
The 49ers-Cowboys rivalry produced some of the most iconic moments in NFL playoff history in the 1980s and 1990s.
The 49ers-Rams rivalry is the oldest in the NFC West, dating back to the 1950s.
The Big Game between Cal and Stanford is one of college football's oldest rivalries.
USC-UCLA football rivalry game has been played annually since 1929.
The Battle of California — Sharks vs. Kings vs. Ducks — intensifies every NHL playoff season.
The Lakers won the 1980 NBA Championship — rookie Magic Johnson played center in Game 6, scoring 42 points.
The Lakers lost the 2004 NBA Finals to the Detroit Pistons in 5 games, ending the Shaq-Kobe era.
The Lakers defeated the Celtics in 7 games to win the 2010 NBA Championship — Kobe won his fifth ring.
Russell Westbrook was traded to the Lakers, reuniting with LeBron James and Anthony Davis.
The Los Angeles Rams defeated the Cleveland Browns to win the 1951 NFL Championship — the franchise's last title in LA until 2022.
The Rams left Los Angeles for St. Louis in 1995.
Sean McVay became the youngest head coach in modern NFL history when hired by the Rams at age 30.
Von Miller stripped the ball from Cam Newton in Super Bowl 50 at Levi's Stadium to clinch the Broncos' victory.
The LA Chargers played their first season in a tiny soccer stadium, the StubHub Center, seating 27,000.
Natalie Coughlin of Cal became the first woman to swim the 100m backstroke in under a minute.
Misty May-Treanor and Kerri Walsh Jennings, both Californians, won three consecutive Olympic beach volleyball gold medals.
The San Francisco Giants celebrated Pride Night, becoming one of the first MLB teams to do so.
The Dodgers' Fernando Valenzuela won both the Cy Young and Rookie of the Year in 1981.
The Dodgers retired Fernando Valenzuela's No. 34.
Fernando Valenzuela passed away at age 63.
The Sacramento Republic FC was announced as an MLS expansion team.
The Oakland Roots SC played their first USL Championship match.
Stanford's Tara VanDerveer became the winningest coach in NCAA women's basketball history.
USC's women's basketball team won back-to-back NCAA titles in 1983 and 1984 under Linda Sharp.
The LA Rams won the inaugural NFL Championship in 1945 while still based in Cleveland.
Tom Brady's final NFL game was a loss to the Rams in the 2021 NFC Divisional Playoff.
Odell Beckham Jr. tore his ACL in Super Bowl LVI while playing for the Rams.
The San Francisco 49ers introduced the read-option with Colin Kaepernick, rushing for 181 yards in a 2012 playoff game.
The LA Galaxy won MLS Cup 2005 over the New England Revolution.
Robbie Keane scored a brace in the 2014 MLS Cup Final for the Galaxy.
Wayne Gretzky scored his 802nd career goal to become the NHL's all-time leading goal scorer.
The Mighty Ducks of Anaheim played their first NHL game.
The Los Angeles Kings drafted Luc Robitaille 171st overall in 1984 — he went on to score 668 career goals.
John Gibson established himself as an elite goaltender for the Anaheim Ducks.
The San Jose Sharks made the NHL playoffs for the first time in their history.
The Sharks upset the Detroit Red Wings in the first round of the 1994 NHL playoffs — one of the biggest upsets in hockey history.
Owen Nolan called his shot against Curtis Joseph in the 1997 NHL All-Star Game, held in San Jose.
The Los Angeles Sparks won the 2003 WNBA Championship with Lisa Leslie as Finals MVP.
Sue Bird and Diana Taurasi — both California high school legends — became the two greatest players in WNBA history.
The 2026 FIFA World Cup will have matches at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles.
The USFL's LA Express signed Steve Young to the richest contract in football history at the time.
The XFL's LA Wildcats played in 2020 before the league suspended operations.
Tony Parker scored 21 points in the 4th quarter of a playoff game against the Warriors.
Stephen Jackson and Baron Davis led the Warriors' 'We Believe' playoff run.
Monta Ellis was traded by the Warriors to the Milwaukee Bucks for Andrew Bogut.
D'Angelo Russell was traded to the Warriors as part of the Kevin Durant sign-and-trade.
The Sacramento Kings hired Mike Brown as head coach, leading a revitalization.
Domantas Sabonis was traded to the Sacramento Kings from the Indiana Pacers.
The Sacramento Kings' Mitch Richmond won the NBA All-Star Game MVP.
The Clippers won a playoff series for the first time since 2006, defeating the Memphis Grizzlies in 2012.
The Warriors' Splash Brothers — Steph Curry and Klay Thompson — combined for 484 three-pointers in the 2015-16 season.
The San Francisco Giants won the 1954 World Series while still in New York, sweeping the Cleveland Indians behind Willie Mays.
The Angels' Troy Glaus was named 2002 World Series MVP.
The Padres' Jake Peavy won the NL Cy Young Award.
The San Diego Chicken (Ted Giannoulas) became one of the most famous mascots in sports history, debuting at Padres games.
UCLA gymnast Katelyn Ohashi scored a perfect 10 on floor exercise, and the video went viral with over 100 million views.
USC's Keyshawn Johnson was drafted first overall by the New York Jets.
Cal's DeSean Jackson returned a punt 65 yards for a touchdown against Big Game rival Stanford, celebrating early and dropping the ball before the end zone.
Long Beach State's men's volleyball program won its first NCAA championship.
Stanford's Brandi Chastain kicked the winning penalty in the 1999 Women's World Cup Final and ripped off her jersey in celebration.
The Anaheim Ducks retired Paul Kariya's No. 9 jersey.
The San Jose Sharks retired Patrick Marleau's No. 12 jersey.
San Diego State football went undefeated in the 2023 regular season under Brady Hoke.
Fresno State's Davante Adams starred for the Bulldogs before becoming an NFL All-Pro receiver.
The LA Rams' Torry Holt caught a 73-yard touchdown pass in Super Bowl XXXIV while in St. Louis.
The Oakland Raiders' Tim Brown won the Heisman Trophy at Notre Dame and starred for the Raiders for 16 seasons.
